summary_strategy: Reframe summary around building and operating production-grade security tooling with emphasis on deployable controls, automation, and cloud delivery — directly mirroring GuidePoint’s pragmatic, mission-driven tone

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, PowerShell, Bash, and Go first (most relevant for automation and cloud tooling), then keep remaining languages in original order
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Add CrowdStrike Falcon, Rapid7 InsightVM, and Zeek from inventory; reorder to lead with vulnerability management, EDR/XDR, then SIEM and SOAR — aligning with remediation automation focus
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Rename section title to 'AI for SecOps' only if allowed; per instructions, keep EXACT name 'AI/ML and Research'; reorder to lead with RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, then NLP and retrieval tuning — emphasizing auditability and operational governance
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI, Docker, and MCP/FastMCP first (core of Coraline tooling), then React.js, Node.js, Django — aligning with internal security tool development and API-driven workflows
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Add Terraform, Ansible, GitHub Actions from inventory; reorder to lead with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure as code, then container orchestration, secrets management, and cloud platforms — matching DevSecOps emphasis
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, SQLite, Redis, vector databases first (used in Coraline and PhishFinder), then keep remaining databases in original order

experience_focus.must_highlight: production-grade tooling delivery, API-driven automation, cloud-native deployment (AWS/Azure), cross-functional security integration
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 7,000+ endpoints, 2,900-user identity portal, 10,000+ devices, 200 hours annually saved, 500 hours annual workload reduction, 25 percent compliance improvement, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no vague claims like 'industry-leading', 'best-in-class', or speculative impact without source grounding

risk_controls: Never invent metrics — only reuse numbers explicitly present in baseline resume
Do not add tools or frameworks unless found in Skills Inventory or baseline experience descriptions
Avoid phrases like 'revolutionized', 'transformed', or corporate fluff — use 'built', 'engineered', 'delivered', 'operationalized'
Do not rename or merge skill categories — preserve exact six-category structure
Never assume responsibilities beyond what is documented in baseline experience bullets
